NASA to Host Media Call with Artemis II Crew on Way Home from Moon

Media will have an opportunity at 9:45 p.m. EDT on Wednesday, April 8, to speak with NASA’s Artemis II crew members as they continue their journey toward Earth during their historic mission around the Moon. The 20-minute virtual news conference will take place with NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman,...

Artemis II's return trajectory provides a rare public communication window that demonstrates NASA's commitment to real-time mission transparency during critical lunar transit phases.

Alaska Airlines' transatlantic 737 MAX deployment demonstrates manufacturer confidence in the aircraft's reliability while maximizing narrowbody economics on thin long-haul routes.

New position paper says repeated uneventful flights should not reduce scrutiny.

Pilots face operational fatigue risks when routine conflict-zone missions breed complacency, undermining the heightened risk management these routes demand.

FAA's aggressive trainee recruitment directly addresses the controller shortage bottleneck that has constrained flight operations and delayed modernization initiatives across the NAS.

Major PX4 drone software vulnerability raises hijacking concerns

A widely used piece of drone software just got a serious cybersecurity wake-up call, and if you operate drones in the US, it’s something you’ll want to pay attention to. more…...

PX4 vulnerability exposure spans thousands of commercial and industrial drone operations, demanding immediate firmware patching across the US drone fleet to prevent coordinated hijacking incidents.

Airlines are capitalizing on premium economy's higher margins and lower cost than business class to diversify revenue streams from price-sensitive but comfort-seeking passengers.

Military Apr 7, 2026

New Details Emerge About Rescue Mission Deep Inside Iran

New details were released during a White House press conference which described the rescue of the crew of DUDE 44, the F-15E Strike Eagle that went down in Iran. The rescue of the crew of the F-15E Strike Eagle fighter jet shot down over Iran has rapidly emerged as one of the most complex and […]...

Complex combat rescue operations in hostile territory require unprecedented coordination between multiple military branches and international partners to ensure aircrew survival.

General Aviation Apr 7, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Mid-sized regional airports like CID serve as critical training hubs where flight schools and air carriers coexist, creating essential pipeline capacity for pilot development and regional air service.

Starfish Space raises more than $100 million

Starfish Space has raised more than $100 million to scale up production of its satellite servicing spacecraft.

Commercial satellite servicing is attracting significant venture capital, signaling investor confidence in on-orbit maintenance as a viable business model to extend spacecraft lifecycles and reduce debris.

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

NASA’s new moon base project requires operational technology systems in space, but they are vulnerable.

Newly anointed NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman made a $20 billion announcement on March 24, 2026. To the delight of space enthusiasts everywhere, Isaacman said NASA was cancelling its project to deploy a space station in lunar orbit and would reorient to building a $20 billion base on the surface...

NASA's shift to a lunar surface base over orbital station prioritizes operational resilience but introduces cybersecurity risks that could compromise mission-critical systems in an austere, irretrievable environment.

See SpaceNews →

Jetstar’s first refurbished Boeing 787 Dreamliner launched its inaugural service from Melbourne Airport (MEL) to Phuket International Airport…...

Jetstar's 787 deployment signals ultra-low-cost carriers are expanding into long-haul markets, forcing legacy carriers to defend premium international routes.

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

British Airways Flew 13 Hours After Passenger Died Onboard - Foul Smell Complaints Followed

You might expect that, when a passenger passes away during a flight, pilots would divert the aircraft to an airport to deal with the situation. However, this isn’t always the case. Indeed, if a passenger dies of natural causes, airlines may just opt to have the flight continue onward to its...

Airlines can legally continue flights after natural-cause passenger deaths, creating operational and passenger experience challenges that airlines must manage without mandatory diversions.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

The Problem With Replacing Aircraft Like The Boeing 747

The biggest problem with replacing the Boeing 747 is simple: no single aircraft can do everything the 747 did.

The 747's unique combination of cargo capacity, passenger range, and cargo-door capability created an irreplaceable operational niche that competing aircraft must serve through multiple types.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Apr 7, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Sustainable aviation requires integrated optimization across all operational layers—not just aircraft technology—to handle projected traffic growth without proportional environmental impact.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Earth observation operators push to deliver satellite images within minutes

Vantor employees were gathered for a sales kickoff in January, when an executive announced that a WorldView Legion satellite passing overhead would snap a photo of the California venue. Later, a buzzer sounded to alert the audience that the 30-centimeter-resolution image was available on the Vantor...

Real-time satellite imagery delivery is shifting from hours to minutes, creating new commercial opportunities for time-sensitive applications like emergency response and infrastructure monitoring.

See SpaceNews →

Military Apr 7, 2026

YFQ-42A Dark Merlin CCA Crashes in California During Test

GA-ASI confirmed a YFQ-42 crashed during take-off and testing has now been paused temporarily, with an investigation already launched. A prototype of the YFQ-42A Dark Merlin Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA), developed by General Atomics Aeronautical Systems, Inc (GA-ASI) crashed at the...

The Dark Merlin CCA crash during early testing highlights the development risks of autonomous combat systems and may delay DoD fielding timelines for collaborative crewed-uncrewed operations.

See The Aviationist →

Australia's proven JORN technology enables Canada to establish persistent Arctic surveillance capability, addressing critical gaps in monitoring remote northern airspace and vessels.

Manna expands drone delivery network with $50M boost

The company announced a fresh $50 million Series B round, bringing its total funding to $110 million, as it prepares to rapidly scale operations across the...

Manna's $50M Series B funding signals investor confidence in autonomous last-mile delivery as a near-term commercial reality, not just a future concept.

Drones Apr 7, 2026

DJI confirms end-of-support timeline for Mavic 2, Matrice 600 drones

Tech giant DJI will officially end service and support for several of its most beloved professional and prosumer drones in the coming weeks. That includes the iconic DJI Mavic 2 Pro, the versatile DJI Mavic 2 Enterprise Zoom and DJI Mavic 2 Enterprise Dual, and the heavy-lift powerhouse DJI Matrice...

DJI's end-of-support timeline signals operators must migrate to newer platforms or face operational gaps in firmware updates and technical assistance.
